feedback.text: I'd like to point out the desperate need for clear guidelines and regulation in the games industry, at the moment it's apperantly completely legal to take away the product you spend money on as a consumer. Take for instance Blizzard's game "Overwatch", this game the consumer paid anything from aroudn 40 - 60 euro for, which was not advertised as a "Rental" or "subscription" but as a full game you paid once for and would have access to all new content they'd make, with optional payments for cosmetics in which you could get through the "lootboxes" (pretty much a slot machine even with flashy lights). However this suddenly ended without warning as they decided to turn their new game "Overwatch 2" form a single/coop game and just terminate "Overwatch", which was a "Free to play" kind of game. (no entree price unlike the first game, and which of course always has manipulative ways that make you spend way more money.) So on october 2022 the "Overwatch" servers were taken down and basically destroyed it forever as there's no way to play it anymore. So everyone who bought Overwatch and dearly enjoyed the game got basically a middle finger as the game they had bought and loved was deleted forever as the replacement made a bunch of changes that for quite a lot of people just made the origional game worse in almost every aspect and there was ( and still isn't) a way to play the orgional one. This is just one of MANY examples where companies take away our access to games we have spend money on without being refunded. (See "The Crew", and how it started the consumer initiative "Stop Killing Games") Companies/publishers are all too eager to revoke access to something consumers have spend money on their sequel, this is an extremely anti-consumer practise! Imagine if for example, applemusic decided to just revoke access to the albums you bought. Or if the carcompany that made your car decided to cancel your access through blocking it from turning on by software? These are the kinds of things this industry is pushing for and it shouldn't be allowed or accepted! Video games are art and these works are being destoryed just for the sake of chasing more profit and if this is accepted one day it'll become the norm for not just video games but all digital media and services, it's already being pushed in other places (a few examples: BMW's seat warmers subscription, Microsofts change from buying "Microsoft Office" to making it a monthly/yearly subscription and removing the option to buy) The EU is the 3rd biggest marketblock in the world, we should set an example that these companies are not allowed to what basically comes down to stealing away consumer rights just to squeeze a bit more money out of everyone! I'd urge the European Commision to please heed our warnings and take action! Thank you for your time.
